content.full_text:
MS. Sheryl R. Falstein 
Jack McGrath and AssOEaates 
1100 Glendon Avenue, Suite 1721 
Los Angeles, CA 
90024 
Dears MS. Falstein: 
A-- ~() -IS-, 
September 23, 1980 
This letter is in response to your request for written 
confirmation of advice I provided to you by telephone. 
During our telephone conversation, and in your letter of 
September 8, 1980, you stated that Jack McGrath and Associates 
was retained by a client to provide public relations and 
campaign consulting services. 
The funds expended by your 
organization amounted to $500 or more and the single source of 
these funds was your client who did not solicit or raise funds 
from any other source. 
~ 
You asked for advice, based on the above facts. regarding 
the reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act. 
I advised you that yourcli.ent is a conmittee pursuant to 
Government Code Section 820J3 (b) and that he is required to 
file appropriate campaign disclosure statements (Form 461). 
Further, I advised you that Jack McGrath and Assocaites is an 
agent or independent contractor, 'pursua~t to Government Code 
Section 84303, for your client, and Jack McGrath and Associates 
should provide your client with an itemization of your expendi-
tures so your client could report them in the same manner as if 
he made those expenditures directly. 
JG/bb 
If you have furhher qaastions, p.aaeeconcaat~i. 
Yours rruly, 
John C. Greenwood 
Consultant

sections.question: You asked for advice, based on the above facts, regarding the reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion: I advised you that your client is a committee pursuant to Government Code Section 82003(b) and that he is required to file appropriate campaign disclosure statements (Form 461). Further, I advised that Jack McGrath and Associates is an agent or independent contractor, pursuant to Government Code Section 84303, for your client, and Jack McGrath and Associates should provide your client with an itemization of your expenditures so your client could report them in the same manner as if he made those expenditures directly.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act when a public relations and campaign consulting firm (Jack McGrath and Associates) expends $500 or more in funds provided by a single client who did not solicit or raise funds from other sources?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The client qualifies as a committee under Government Code Section 82003(b) and must file campaign disclosure statements (Form 461). Jack McGrath and Associates acts as an agent/independent contractor and must provide an itemized accounting of expenditures to the client for reporting purposes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a confirmation letter of prior telephonic advice. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present, but question and conclusion content are clearly identifiable in the letter body. Some OCR errors present ('furhther qaastions', 'p.aaeeconcaat~i'). Dated September 23, 1980; references Government Code sections 82003(b) and 84303.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked for advice, based on the above facts, regarding the reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: I advised you that your client is a committee pursuant to Government Code Section 82003(b) and that he is required to file appropriate campaign disclosure statements (Form 461). Further, I advised that Jack McGrath and Associates is an agent or independent contractor, pursuant to Government Code Section 84303, for your client, and Jack McGrath and Associates should provide your client with an itemization of your expenditures so your client could report them in the same manner as if he made those expenditures directly.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephonic advice regarding reporting obligations when a consulting firm retained by a client expends $500 or more in campaign-related funds. The client is deemed a committee requiring Form 461 filings, with the consulting firm acting as an agent providing itemized expenditure reports.
